title: Ecuador FIFA World Cup 2026 Preview: Solid in defence, El Tri are primed to qualify out of group
original_text: Ecuador FIFA World Cup 2026 Preview: Solid in defence, El Tri are primed to qualify out of group left FILE PHOTO: Moises Caicedo will be vital cog in midfield for Ecuador at the World Cup. REUTERS https://ss-i.thgim.com/public/incoming/g7kyo5/article71073906.ece/alternates/FREE_1200/2026-06-03T095559Z_1968348574_RC2I4R97WNNO_RTRMADP_3_SOCCER-WORLDCUP-GROUPE-ECUADOR.JPG JENNIFER LORENZINI FILE PHOTO: Soccer Football - FIFA World Cup Qatar 2022 - Group A - Ecuador v Senegal - Khalifa International Stadium, Doha, Qatar - November 29, 2022 Ecuador's Moises Caicedo celebrates scoring their first goal REUTERS/Jennifer Lorenzini/File Photo El Tri let in just five goals in 18 matches during World Cup qualifying, the fewest among CONMEBOL sides, and will look to frustrate the bigger sides at the showpiece. Chennai Ecuador will come into the World Cup as a firm favourite to make it to the next round. Though the team’s offence is not as menacing as Argentina or Colombia — the South American sides with most goals in the 2026 World Cup qualifiers — its defence has been clinical. The partnership between Willian Pacho and Piero Hincapie, complemented by the experience of Moises Caicedo, has provided the team with a perfect triumvirate to stall counterattacks from the opponents. In the UEFA Champions League this season, Caicedo had the most tackles (41) while Pacho had the most ball recoveries (105). World Cup 2026 - Full coverage https://sportstar.thehindu.com/football/fifa-world-cup/ Unsurprisingly, in its qualification campaign, El Tri let in just five goals in 18 matches, the fewest among CONMEBOL sides, and finished second in the qualifiers, just behind Argentina, losing only to the World champion and Brazil in the entire campaign. And if Ecuador’s pragmatism becomes too predictable, add the seasoning of attacks by youngster Kendry Paez and the seasoned runs of Enner Valencia to complete a well-balanced squad. Paez, who became the youngest goalscorer in the Ecuadorian top flight at 15, will hope to turn heads at the World Cup as one of the most sought-after prodigies in the world. Players to watch out for: Willian Pacho Alex Ferguson had once said, ‘Attack wins you games, defence wins you titles’. It is no surprise that when Paris Saint-Germain won consecutive Champions League titles, Pacho was one of the key players at the back. The World Cup has seldom, like in 2006, elevated defenders to the status of legends, and Pacho puts a strong case for Ecuador in that aspect. Manager: Sebastian Beccacece Assistant managers have often gone on to have bright careers as head coaches. Ask Carlo Ancelotti, who assisted Arrigo Sacchi at the 1994 World Cup, or Hansi Flick, who shared the dugout with Joachim Low at the 2014 edition of the quadrennial tournament. center FILE PHOTO: Ecuador coach Sebastian Beccacece was an assistant to Jorge Sampaoli in the 2014 and 2018 editions. CESAR OLMEDO REUTERS https://ss-i.thgim.com/public/incoming/txvhdf/article71073896.ece/alternates/FREE_1200/2026-06-03T095509Z_51887420_RC29LGAKPASN_RTRMADP_3_SOCCER-WORLDCUP-GROUPE-ECUADOR.JPG CESAR OLMEDO FILE PHOTO: Soccer Football - World Cup - CONMEBOL Qualifiers - Paraguay v Ecuador - Estadio Defensores del Chaco, Asuncion, Paraguay - September 4, 2025 Ecuador coach Sebastian Beccacece reacts REUTERS/Cesar Olmedo/File Photo Sebastián Beccacece, who was Jorge Sampaoli’s assistant at the 2014 and 2018 World Cups, will hope to tread a similar path, taking charge of a national team in the tournaments for the first time. The 45-year-old has been in charge of Ecuador since 2024 and oversaw an impressive qualification campaign. And in Mexico, Canada and the USA, he will look to extend the purple match to inspire the team past the group stage for the first time since 2006.
